name: Deel API
what_it_does: Deel's API manages global contractor agreements, full-time employee onboarding, payroll processing, and compliance in 150+ countries — enabling automated workforce and payment workflows.
use_cases: Automating contractor onboarding and agreement generation for global freelancers
Syncing Deel payroll data with internal HRIS and accounting systems
Monitoring payment status and contractor compliance from agent dashboards
Triggering off-cycle payments and adjustments through automated workflows
Generating workforce analytics (headcount, cost-by-country) from agent pipelines
not_for: Small businesses without international hiring needs — Gusto or Rippling are simpler for US-only
Real-time payroll calculations (Deel is a managed service, not a payroll calculation engine)
Replacing a full HRIS — Deel focuses on global compliance and payments, not performance management
best_when: Your company hires international contractors or employees in multiple countries and needs automated compliance, agreements, and payroll from a single API.
avoid_when: You only hire in one country and need basic payroll — Deel's complexity and cost is overkill for single-country payroll.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 for third-party integrations; personal access tokens for direct API access. Scopes map to Deel features (contracts, payments, people). API requires Deel Enterprise or Scale plan.

pricing.free_tier_limits: API access requires paid Deel account
pricing.paid_tiers: Contractor plans from $49/month; EOR from $599/month. API access on Scale/Enterprise plans.
pricing.notes: Deel pricing is per-employee/contractor. API access is not a separate line item — included with plan. Enterprise contracts for large teams.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; contact Deel for limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST errors follow standard patterns but compliance-related rejections (KYC, contract validation) return generic codes. Webhook events for async payroll state changes. Developer docs less comprehensive than pure API companies.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Some endpoints support idempotency keys; contract creation should be checked for duplicates before retry. Payment requests are not automatically idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Contract lifecycle has many states (draft, pending, active, ended) — agents must implement a state machine for workflow automation
Compliance rules vary by country — what's valid in the US may be rejected for Brazil or Germany workers
Payment timing depends on Deel's processing schedule — agents cannot trigger instant payments outside of pay run windows
Webhook payloads require signature verification — missing this is a security risk
API documentation trails the product — some endpoints in use may not be in official docs

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001 certified. OAuth 2.0 for integrations. Webhook signature validation available. Personal data handling requires GDPR compliance. Multi-region data handling for global workforce.
